draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to friendly, conversational tone than formal corporate language
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Warm and casual
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer approachable, human-centered voice; copy should feel like talking to a peer, not reading documentation
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer professional, authoritative voice; copy should establish credibility and competence through formal tone

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to friendly, conversational tone than formal corporate language
evidence.implication: Users prefer approachable, human-centered voice; copy should feel like talking to a peer, not reading documentation

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers working late prefer interfaces with high contrast and reduced eye strain
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark-first design, high contrast palettes, reduced brightness for evening workflows
facade.rejectImplies: Pursue bright, airy interfaces with natural light aesthetics, possibly light mode default

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer CRM data that refreshes instantly without waiting for page loads or sync delays
facade.label: Instant updates
facade.acceptImplies: User values snappy, reactive feedback; likely prefers click-based navigation and immediate visual confirmation of actions
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer smooth, orchestrated transitions between states; animation and motion could signal reliability and thoughtfulness to them

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers works better with calm, desaturated palettes that reduce cognitive load during long work sessions
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool / calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft, muted colors
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward restful, low-contrast interfaces—likely values focus and reduced visual fatigue
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer vibrant, high-contrast, or punchy aesthetics—values clarity and visual hierarchy through boldness

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent project navigation alongside flexible task viewing
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure (sidebar vs full-width) + information density (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with floating cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick project switching and prefers modular card-based layouts for tasks/contacts
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer linear tab-based navigation or dense list views without spatial separation

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer at-a-glance metrics (revenue, active clients, pending invoices) over detailed narrative reports
facade.label: Dashboard with big numbers
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick status scanning and KPI-focused design; likely wants prominent financial overview on entry
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers narrative context, trend explanation, or detailed breakdowns over isolated metrics; may favor charts or prose summaries

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human, supportive guidance
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
Whether they want data-heavy metrics and analytics vs. simple, task-focused summaries—reveals if they need deep insights or just enough to act
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and flow

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human, supportive guidance
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want data-heavy metrics and analytics vs. simple, task-focused summaries—reveals if they need deep insights or just enough to act
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and flow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers value professionalism and credibility over personality; a stripped-down, serious tone builds trust and legitimacy
facade.label: All business, no humor
facade.acceptImplies: Strip personality from copy; use formal language, technical accuracy, and corporate polish to signal reliability
facade.rejectImplies: Personality and warmth are non-negotiable; even in a productivity tool, freelancers want to feel seen as humans, not just billing units
